== History ==
According to temple records, Renjō-ji was founded during the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] under the imperial decree of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. It was later rebuilt in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] by {{ill|Ki no Aritsune|ja|紀有常|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Aritsune|qq=}}.<ref name="kenshi">Iwakiri Takatoshi, Oya Yoshinori, eds., "Nara Prefecture History Vol. 6: Temples," Meicho Shuppan, 1991, p.273.</ref><ref name="shishi">Nara City History Editorial Council, "Nara City History: Temples Edition," Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1985, p.125.</ref>
It is also believed to be associated with the remains of {{ill|Ki-dera Temple|ja|紀寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki-dera Temple|qq=}}, evidenced by the discovery of early Nara period tiles in the surrounding area.<ref name="shishi" />
In 1602, [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted Renjō-ji land worth 20 koku in Chōtsuka and Hokkeji villages, a temple domain maintained throughout the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].<ref name="kenshi" /><ref name="heibon">"Japanese Historical Place Names Series Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture," Heibonsha, 1981, p.520.</ref>
The temple was initially affiliated with the [[:en:East Asian Yogācāra|Hossō sect]], later becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Seigan-ji|ja|誓願寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seigan-ji|qq=}} of the [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo sect]]. In 1725, it converted to [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Buddhism]], becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Yōgen-in|ja|養源院|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōgen-in|qq=}} in Kyoto.<ref name="shishi" /> Currently, Renjō-ji belongs to the Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect and operates without parishioners.<ref name="shishi" />
Although it experienced decline after the Meiji Restoration, Renjō-ji has since been restored, with an expanded cemetery and maintained temple grounds.<ref name="shishi" /> In 1964, a large {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|qq=}} statue was erected to honor war casualties.<ref name="shishi" />
== Grounds ==
The temple features a main gate, a kitchen building to the left, and a guest hall beyond the central gate.<ref name="shishi" /> Several stone statues of ancient Buddhas are visible near the central gate and to the right of the main hall.<ref name="shishi" />
The central object of worship is a white, unclothed standing statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], adorned with a fabric hakama. This sacred image is unveiled annually in May.<ref name="shishi" /><ref name="heibon" />
Flanking the main deity are standing statues of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] (Heian period) and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Seishi Bosatsu]] (Muromachi period), both designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].<ref name="heibon" /><ref name="shishi" />

== Renjō-ji (璉珹寺) ==
'''Renjō-ji''' (璉珹寺) is a temple of the {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect|ja|浄土真宗遣迎院派|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect|qq=}} located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan.
== History ==
According to temple records, Renjō-ji was founded during the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] under the imperial decree of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. It was later rebuilt in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] by {{ill|Ki no Aritsune|ja|紀有常|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Aritsune|qq=}}.<ref name="kenshi">Iwakiri Takatoshi, Oya Yoshinori, eds., "Nara Prefecture History Vol. 6: Temples," Meicho Shuppan, 1991, p.273.</ref><ref name="shishi">Nara City History Editorial Council, "Nara City History: Temples Edition," Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1985, p.125.</ref>
It is also believed to be associated with the remains of {{ill|Ki-dera Temple|ja|紀寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki-dera Temple|qq=}}, evidenced by the discovery of early Nara period tiles in the surrounding area.<ref name="shishi" />
In 1602, [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted Renjō-ji land worth 20 koku in Chōtsuka and Hokkeji villages, a temple domain maintained throughout the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].<ref name="kenshi" /><ref name="heibon">"Japanese Historical Place Names Series Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture," Heibonsha, 1981, p.520.</ref>
The temple was initially affiliated with the [[:en:East Asian Yogācāra|Hossō sect]], later becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Seigan-ji|ja|誓願寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seigan-ji|qq=}} of the [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo sect]]. In 1725, it converted to [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Buddhism]], becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Yōgen-in|ja|養源院|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōgen-in|qq=}} in Kyoto.<ref name="shishi" /> Currently, Renjō-ji belongs to the Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect and operates without parishioners.<ref name="shishi" />
Although it experienced decline after the Meiji Restoration, Renjō-ji has since been restored, with an expanded cemetery and maintained temple grounds.<ref name="shishi" /> In 1964, a large {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|qq=}} statue was erected to honor war casualties.<ref name="shishi" />
== Grounds ==
The temple features a main gate, a kitchen building to the left, and a guest hall beyond the central gate.<ref name="shishi" /> Several stone statues of ancient Buddhas are visible near the central gate and to the right of the main hall.<ref name="shishi" />
The central object of worship is a white, unclothed standing statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], adorned with a fabric hakama. This sacred image is unveiled annually in May.<ref name="shishi" /><ref name="heibon" />
Flanking the main deity are standing statues of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] (Heian period) and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Seishi Bosatsu]] (Muromachi period), both designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].<ref name="heibon" /><ref name="shishi" />
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] * Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Seishi Bosatsu]]
=== Other Cultural Properties ===
* Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] (designated tangible cultural property of Nara Prefecture)
== Related Sites ==
* {{ill|Ki-dera|ja|紀寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki-dera|qq=}} * {{ill|Sudo Tennō-sha|ja|崇道天皇社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudo Tennō-sha|qq=}} (Guardian Shrine) * {{ill|Shōgaku-ji (Nara)|ja|正覚寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgaku-ji (Nara)|qq=}} (Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect) * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|ja|御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}

'''Renjō-ji''' (璉珹寺) is a temple of the {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect|ja|浄土真宗遣迎院派|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect|qq=}} located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan.
== History ==
According to temple records, Renjō-ji was founded during the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] under the imperial decree of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. It was later rebuilt in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] by {{ill|Ki no Aritsune|ja|紀有常|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Aritsune|qq=}}.<ref name="kenshi">Iwakiri Takatoshi, Oya Yoshinori, eds., "Nara Prefecture History Vol. 6: Temples," Meicho Shuppan, 1991, p.273.</ref><ref name="shishi">Nara City History Editorial Council, "Nara City History: Temples Edition," Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1985, p.125.</ref>
It is also believed to be associated with the remains of {{ill|Ki-dera Temple|ja|紀寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki-dera Temple|qq=}}, evidenced by the discovery of early Nara period tiles in the surrounding area.<ref name="shishi" />
In 1602, [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted Renjō-ji land worth 20 koku in Chōtsuka and Hokkeji villages, a temple domain maintained throughout the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].<ref name="kenshi" /><ref name="heibon">"Japanese Historical Place Names Series Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture," Heibonsha, 1981, p.520.</ref>
The temple was initially affiliated with the [[:en:East Asian Yogācāra|Hossō sect]], later becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Seigan-ji|ja|誓願寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seigan-ji|qq=}} of the [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo sect]]. In 1725, it converted to [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Buddhism]], becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Yōgen-in|ja|養源院|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōgen-in|qq=}} in Kyoto.<ref name="shishi" /> Currently, Renjō-ji belongs to the Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect and operates without parishioners.<ref name="shishi" />
Although it experienced decline after the Meiji Restoration, Renjō-ji has since been restored, with an expanded cemetery and maintained temple grounds.<ref name="shishi" /> In 1964, a large {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|qq=}} statue was erected to honor war casualties.<ref name="shishi" />
== Grounds ==
The temple features a main gate, a kitchen building to the left, and a guest hall beyond the central gate.<ref name="shishi" /> Several stone statues of ancient Buddhas are visible near the central gate and to the right of the main hall.<ref name="shishi" />
The central object of worship is a white, unclothed standing statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], adorned with a fabric hakama. This sacred image is unveiled annually in May.<ref name="shishi" /><ref name="heibon" />
Flanking the main deity are standing statues of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] (Heian period) and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Seishi Bosatsu]] (Muromachi period), both designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].<ref name="heibon" /><ref name="shishi" />
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] * Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Seishi Bosatsu]]
=== Other Cultural Properties ===
* Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] (designated tangible cultural property of Nara Prefecture)
== Related Sites ==

In 1725, it converted to [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Buddhism]], becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Yōgen-in|ja|養源院|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōgen-in|qq=}} in Kyoto.<ref name="shishi" /> Currently, Renjō-ji belongs to the Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect and operates without parishioners.<ref name="shishi" />
Although it experienced decline after the Meiji Restoration, Renjō-ji has since been restored, with an expanded cemetery and maintained temple grounds.<ref name="shishi" /> In 1964, a large {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 観音菩薩}} statue was erected to honor war casualties.<ref name="shishi" />
== Grounds ==
The temple features a main gate, a kitchen building to the left, and a guest hall beyond the central gate.<ref name="shishi" /> Several stone statues of ancient Buddhas are visible near the central gate and to the right of the main hall.<ref name="shishi" />
The central object of worship is a white, unclothed standing statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], adorned with a fabric hakama. This sacred image is unveiled annually in May.<ref name="shishi" /><ref name="heibon" />
Flanking the main deity are standing statues of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] (Heian period) and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Seishi Bosatsu]] (Muromachi period), both designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].<ref name="heibon" /><ref name="shishi" />

revision.text:
== Renjō-ji (璉珹寺) ==
'''Renjō-ji''' (璉珹寺) is a temple of the {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect|ja|浄土真宗遣迎院派|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect|qq=}} located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan.
== History ==
According to temple records, Renjō-ji was founded during the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] under the imperial decree of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. It was later rebuilt in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] by {{ill|Ki no Aritsune|ja|紀有常|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Aritsune|qq=}}.<ref name="kenshi">Iwakiri Takatoshi, Oya Yoshinori, eds., "Nara Prefecture History Vol. 6: Temples," Meicho Shuppan, 1991, p.273.</ref><ref name="shishi">Nara City History Editorial Council, "Nara City History: Temples Edition," Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1985, p.125.</ref>
It is also believed to be associated with the remains of {{ill|Ki-dera Temple|ja|紀寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki-dera Temple|qq=}}, evidenced by the discovery of early Nara period tiles in the surrounding area.<ref name="shishi" />
In 1602, [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted Renjō-ji land worth 20 koku in Chōtsuka and Hokkeji villages, a temple domain maintained throughout the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].<ref name="kenshi" /><ref name="heibon">"Japanese Historical Place Names Series Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture," Heibonsha, 1981, p.520.</ref>
The temple was initially affiliated with the [[:en:East Asian Yogācāra|Hossō sect]], later becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Seigan-ji|ja|誓願寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seigan-ji|qq=}} of the [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo sect]]. In 1725, it converted to [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Buddhism]], becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Yōgen-in|ja|養源院|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōgen-in|qq=}} in Kyoto.<ref name="shishi" /> Currently, Renjō-ji belongs to the Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect and operates without parishioners.<ref name="shishi" />
Although it experienced decline after the Meiji Restoration, Renjō-ji has since been restored, with an expanded cemetery and maintained temple grounds.<ref name="shishi" /> In 1964, a large {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 観音菩薩}} statue was erected to honor war casualties.<ref name="shishi" />
== Grounds ==
The temple features a main gate, a kitchen building to the left, and a guest hall beyond the central gate.<ref name="shishi" /> Several stone statues of ancient Buddhas are visible near the central gate and to the right of the main hall.<ref name="shishi" />
The central object of worship is a white, unclothed standing statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], adorned with a fabric hakama. This sacred image is unveiled annually in May.<ref name="shishi" /><ref name="heibon" />
Flanking the main deity are standing statues of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] (Heian period) and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Seishi Bosatsu]] (Muromachi period), both designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].<ref name="heibon" /><ref name="shishi" />
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] * Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Seishi Bosatsu]]
=== Other Cultural Properties ===
* Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] (designated tangible cultural property of Nara Prefecture)
== Related Sites ==
* {{ill|Ki-dera|ja|紀寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki-dera|qq=}} * {{ill|Sudo Tennō-sha|ja|崇道天皇社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudo Tennō-sha|qq=}} (Guardian Shrine) * {{ill|Shōgaku-ji (Nara)|ja|正覚寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgaku-ji (Nara)|qq=}} (Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect) * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|ja|御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==
* [https://renjoji.or.jp/ja/ Official Website of Renjō-ji] * [https://yaokami.jp/1295144/ Yaokami - Information on Japanese Shrines and Temples]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22120877]] Wikidata name: Renjō-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:25, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} == Renjō-ji (璉珹寺) ==
'''Renjō-ji''' (璉珹寺) is a temple of the {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect|ja|浄土真宗遣迎院派|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect|qq=}} located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan.
== History ==
According to temple records, Renjō-ji was founded during the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] under the imperial decree of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. It was later rebuilt in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] by {{ill|Ki no Aritsune|ja|紀有常|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Aritsune|qq=}}.<ref name="kenshi">Iwakiri Takatoshi, Oya Yoshinori, eds., "Nara Prefecture History Vol. 6: Temples," Meicho Shuppan, 1991, p.273.</ref><ref name="shishi">Nara City History Editorial Council, "Nara City History: Temples Edition," Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1985, p.125.</ref>
It is also believed to be associated with the remains of {{ill|Ki-dera Temple|ja|紀寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki-dera Temple|qq=}}, evidenced by the discovery of early Nara period tiles in the surrounding area.<ref name="shishi" />
In 1602, [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted Renjō-ji land worth 20 koku in Chōtsuka and Hokkeji villages, a temple domain maintained throughout the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].<ref name="kenshi" /><ref name="heibon">"Japanese Historical Place Names Series Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture," Heibonsha, 1981, p.520.</ref>
The temple was initially affiliated with the [[:en:East Asian Yogācāra|Hossō sect]], later becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Seigan-ji|ja|誓願寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seigan-ji|qq=}} of the [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo sect]]. In 1725, it converted to [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Buddhism]], becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Yōgen-in|ja|養源院|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōgen-in|qq=}} in Kyoto.<ref name="shishi" /> Currently, Renjō-ji belongs to the Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect and operates without parishioners.<ref name="shishi" />
Although it experienced decline after the Meiji Restoration, Renjō-ji has since been restored, with an expanded cemetery and maintained temple grounds.<ref name="shishi" /> In 1964, a large {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 観音菩薩}} statue was erected to honor war casualties.<ref name="shishi" />
== Grounds ==
The temple features a main gate, a kitchen building to the left, and a guest hall beyond the central gate.<ref name="shishi" /> Several stone statues of ancient Buddhas are visible near the central gate and to the right of the main hall.<ref name="shishi" />
The central object of worship is a white, unclothed standing statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], adorned with a fabric hakama. This sacred image is unveiled annually in May.<ref name="shishi" /><ref name="heibon" />
Flanking the main deity are standing statues of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] (Heian period) and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Seishi Bosatsu]] (Muromachi period), both designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].<ref name="heibon" /><ref name="shishi" />
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] * Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Seishi Bosatsu]]
=== Other Cultural Properties ===
* Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] (designated tangible cultural property of Nara Prefecture)
== Related Sites ==
* {{ill|Ki-dera|ja|紀寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki-dera|qq=}} * {{ill|Sudo Tennō-sha|ja|崇道天皇社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudo Tennō-sha|qq=}} (Guardian Shrine) * {{ill|Shōgaku-ji (Nara)|ja|正覚寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgaku-ji (Nara)|qq=}} (Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect) * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|ja|御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}
== References ==

== External Links ==

[[ja:璉珹寺]] [[Category:chokugan-ji]] [[Category:Buddhist temples in Nara, Nara]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Nara City]] [[Category:Heijō-kyō]] [[Category:Jōdo Shinsu Kangō-in branch]] [[Category:Ki clan]] [[Category:Gyōki]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Renjō-ji (Nishikiderachō, Nara)}}

{{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Nara Renjoji.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Tendai|qid=Q855964}}|prefecture={{ill|Nara|qid=Q169134}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.67333333333333|N|135.8331388888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22120877]] Wikidata name: Renjō-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:25, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} == Renjō-ji (璉珹寺) ==
'''Renjō-ji''' (璉珹寺) is a temple of the {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect|ja|浄土真宗遣迎院派|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect|qq=|1=Jōdo Shinshū Kengōin-ha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556825}} located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan.
== History ==
According to temple records, Renjō-ji was founded during the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] under the imperial decree of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. It was later rebuilt in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] by {{ill|Ki no Aritsune|ja|紀有常|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Aritsune|qq=|1=Ki no Aritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605067}}.<ref name="kenshi">Iwakiri Takatoshi, Oya Yoshinori, eds., "Nara Prefecture History Vol. 6: Temples," Meicho Shuppan, 1991, p.273.</ref><ref name="shishi">Nara City History Editorial Council, "Nara City History: Temples Edition," Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1985, p.125.</ref>
It is also believed to be associated with the remains of {{ill|Ki-dera Temple|ja|紀寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki-dera Temple|qq=|1=kinotera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605022}}, evidenced by the discovery of early Nara period tiles in the surrounding area.<ref name="shishi" />
In 1602, [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted Renjō-ji land worth 20 koku in Chōtsuka and Hokkeji villages, a temple domain maintained throughout the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].<ref name="kenshi" /><ref name="heibon">"Japanese Historical Place Names Series Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture," Heibonsha, 1981, p.520.</ref>
The temple was initially affiliated with the [[:en:East Asian Yogācāra|Hossō sect]], later becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Seigan-ji|ja|誓願寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seigan-ji|qq=|1=Seigan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11028481}} of the [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo sect]]. In 1725, it converted to [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Buddhism]], becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Yōgen-in|ja|養源院|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōgen-in|qq=|1=Yōgen-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666843}} in Kyoto.<ref name="shishi" /> Currently, Renjō-ji belongs to the Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect and operates without parishioners.<ref name="shishi" />
Although it experienced decline after the Meiji Restoration, Renjō-ji has since been restored, with an expanded cemetery and maintained temple grounds.<ref name="shishi" /> In 1964, a large {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 観音菩薩}} statue was erected to honor war casualties.<ref name="shishi" />
== Grounds ==
The temple features a main gate, a kitchen building to the left, and a guest hall beyond the central gate.<ref name="shishi" /> Several stone statues of ancient Buddhas are visible near the central gate and to the right of the main hall.<ref name="shishi" />
The central object of worship is a white, unclothed standing statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], adorned with a fabric hakama. This sacred image is unveiled annually in May.<ref name="shishi" /><ref name="heibon" />
Flanking the main deity are standing statues of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] (Heian period) and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Seishi Bosatsu]] (Muromachi period), both designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].<ref name="heibon" /><ref name="shishi" />
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] * Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Seishi Bosatsu]]
=== Other Cultural Properties ===
* Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] (designated tangible cultural property of Nara Prefecture)
== Related Sites ==
* {{ill|Ki-dera|ja|紀寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki-dera|qq=|1=kinotera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605022}} * {{ill|Sudo Tennō-sha|ja|崇道天皇社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudo Tennō-sha|qq=|1=Sudō Tennō-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119116}} (Guardian Shrine) * {{ill|Shōgaku-ji (Nara)|ja|正覚寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgaku-ji (Nara)|qq=|1=Shōkaku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545636}} (Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect) * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|ja|御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==
* [https://renjoji.or.jp/ja/ Official Website of Renjō-ji] * [https://yaokami.jp/1295144/ Yaokami - Information on Japanese Shrines and Temples]

In 1725, it converted to [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Buddhism]], becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Yōgen-in|ja|養源院|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōgen-in|qq=|1=Yōgen-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666843}} in Kyoto.<ref name="shishi" /> Currently, Renjō-ji belongs to the Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect and operates without parishioners.<ref name="shishi" />
Although it experienced decline after the Meiji Restoration, Renjō-ji has since been restored, with an expanded cemetery and maintained temple grounds.<ref name="shishi" /> In 1964, a large {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 観音菩薩}} statue was erected to honor war casualties.<ref name="shishi" />
== Grounds ==
The temple features a main gate, a kitchen building to the left, and a guest hall beyond the central gate.<ref name="shishi" /> Several stone statues of ancient Buddhas are visible near the central gate and to the right of the main hall.<ref name="shishi" />
The central object of worship is a white, unclothed standing statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], adorned with a fabric hakama. This sacred image is unveiled annually in May.<ref name="shishi" /><ref name="heibon" />
Flanking the main deity are standing statues of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] (Heian period) and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Seishi Bosatsu]] (Muromachi period), both designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].<ref name="heibon" /><ref name="shishi" />

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Renjō-ji (Nishikiderachō, Nara)]][[ja:璉珹寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Nara Renjoji.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Tendai|WD=Q855964}}|prefecture={{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.67333333333333|N|135.8331388888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22120877]] Wikidata name: Renjō-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:25, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} == Renjō-ji (璉珹寺) ==
'''Renjō-ji''' (璉珹寺) is a temple of the {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect|ja|浄土真宗遣迎院派|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect|1=Jōdo Shinshū Kengōin-ha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556825}} located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan.
== History ==
According to temple records, Renjō-ji was founded during the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] under the imperial decree of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. It was later rebuilt in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] by {{ill|Ki no Aritsune|ja|紀有常|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Aritsune|1=Ki no Aritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605067}}.<ref name="kenshi">Iwakiri Takatoshi, Oya Yoshinori, eds., "Nara Prefecture History Vol. 6: Temples," Meicho Shuppan, 1991, p.273.</ref><ref name="shishi">Nara City History Editorial Council, "Nara City History: Temples Edition," Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1985, p.125.</ref>
It is also believed to be associated with the remains of {{ill|Ki-dera Temple|ja|紀寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki-dera Temple|1=kinotera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605022}}, evidenced by the discovery of early Nara period tiles in the surrounding area.<ref name="shishi" />
In 1602, [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted Renjō-ji land worth 20 koku in Chōtsuka and Hokkeji villages, a temple domain maintained throughout the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].<ref name="kenshi" /><ref name="heibon">"Japanese Historical Place Names Series Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture," Heibonsha, 1981, p.520.</ref>
The temple was initially affiliated with the [[:en:East Asian Yogācāra|Hossō sect]], later becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Seigan-ji|ja|誓願寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seigan-ji|1=Seigan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11028481}} of the [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo sect]]. In 1725, it converted to [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Buddhism]], becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Yōgen-in|ja|養源院|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōgen-in|1=Yōgen-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666843}} in Kyoto.<ref name="shishi" /> Currently, Renjō-ji belongs to the Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect and operates without parishioners.<ref name="shishi" />
Although it experienced decline after the Meiji Restoration, Renjō-ji has since been restored, with an expanded cemetery and maintained temple grounds.<ref name="shishi" /> In 1964, a large {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 観音菩薩}} statue was erected to honor war casualties.<ref name="shishi" />
== Grounds ==
The temple features a main gate, a kitchen building to the left, and a guest hall beyond the central gate.<ref name="shishi" /> Several stone statues of ancient Buddhas are visible near the central gate and to the right of the main hall.<ref name="shishi" />
The central object of worship is a white, unclothed standing statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], adorned with a fabric hakama. This sacred image is unveiled annually in May.<ref name="shishi" /><ref name="heibon" />
Flanking the main deity are standing statues of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] (Heian period) and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Seishi Bosatsu]] (Muromachi period), both designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].<ref name="heibon" /><ref name="shishi" />
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] * Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Seishi Bosatsu]]
=== Other Cultural Properties ===
* Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] (designated tangible cultural property of Nara Prefecture)
== Related Sites ==
* {{ill|Ki-dera|ja|紀寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki-dera|1=kinotera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605022}} * {{ill|Sudo Tennō-sha|ja|崇道天皇社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudo Tennō-sha|1=Sudō Tennō-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119116}} (Guardian Shrine) * {{ill|Shōgaku-ji (Nara)|ja|正覚寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgaku-ji (Nara)|1=Shōkaku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545636}} (Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect) * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|ja|御霊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}

* Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] * Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Seishi Bosatsu]]
=== Other Cultural Properties ===
* Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] (designated tangible cultural property of Nara Prefecture)

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Renjō-ji (Nishikiderachō, Nara)]][[ja:璉珹寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Nara Renjoji.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Tendai|WD=Q855964}}|prefecture={{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.67333333333333|N|135.8331388888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22120877]] Wikidata name: Renjō-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:25, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} == Renjō-ji (璉珹寺) ==
'''Renjō-ji''' (璉珹寺) is a temple of the {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect|ja|浄土真宗遣迎院派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect|1=Jōdo Shinshū Kengōin-ha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556825}} located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan.
== History ==
According to temple records, Renjō-ji was founded during the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] under the imperial decree of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. It was later rebuilt in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] by {{ill|Ki no Aritsune|ja|紀有常|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Aritsune|1=Ki no Aritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605067}}.<ref name="kenshi">Iwakiri Takatoshi, Oya Yoshinori, eds., "Nara Prefecture History Vol. 6: Temples," Meicho Shuppan, 1991, p.273.</ref><ref name="shishi">Nara City History Editorial Council, "Nara City History: Temples Edition," Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1985, p.125.</ref>
It is also believed to be associated with the remains of {{ill|Ki-dera Temple|ja|紀寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki-dera Temple|1=kinotera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605022}}, evidenced by the discovery of early Nara period tiles in the surrounding area.<ref name="shishi" />
In 1602, [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted Renjō-ji land worth 20 koku in Chōtsuka and Hokkeji villages, a temple domain maintained throughout the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].<ref name="kenshi" /><ref name="heibon">"Japanese Historical Place Names Series Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture," Heibonsha, 1981, p.520.</ref>
The temple was initially affiliated with the [[:en:East Asian Yogācāra|Hossō sect]], later becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Seigan-ji|ja|誓願寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seigan-ji|1=Seigan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11028481}} of the [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo sect]]. In 1725, it converted to [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Buddhism]], becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Yōgen-in|ja|養源院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōgen-in|1=Yōgen-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666843}} in Kyoto.<ref name="shishi" /> Currently, Renjō-ji belongs to the Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect and operates without parishioners.<ref name="shishi" />
Although it experienced decline after the Meiji Restoration, Renjō-ji has since been restored, with an expanded cemetery and maintained temple grounds.<ref name="shishi" /> In 1964, a large {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 観音菩薩}} statue was erected to honor war casualties.<ref name="shishi" />
== Grounds ==
The temple features a main gate, a kitchen building to the left, and a guest hall beyond the central gate.<ref name="shishi" /> Several stone statues of ancient Buddhas are visible near the central gate and to the right of the main hall.<ref name="shishi" />
The central object of worship is a white, unclothed standing statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], adorned with a fabric hakama. This sacred image is unveiled annually in May.<ref name="shishi" /><ref name="heibon" />
Flanking the main deity are standing statues of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] (Heian period) and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Seishi Bosatsu]] (Muromachi period), both designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].<ref name="heibon" /><ref name="shishi" />
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] * Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Seishi Bosatsu]]
=== Other Cultural Properties ===
* Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] (designated tangible cultural property of Nara Prefecture)
== Related Sites ==
* {{ill|Ki-dera|ja|紀寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki-dera|1=kinotera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605022}} * {{ill|Sudo Tennō-sha|ja|崇道天皇社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudo Tennō-sha|1=Sudō Tennō-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119116}} (Guardian Shrine) * {{ill|Shōgaku-ji (Nara)|ja|正覚寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgaku-ji (Nara)|1=Shōkaku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545636}} (Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect) * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==

In 1725, it converted to [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Buddhism]], becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Yōgen-in|ja|養源院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōgen-in|1=Yōgen-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666843}} in Kyoto.<ref name="shishi" /> Currently, Renjō-ji belongs to the Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect and operates without parishioners.<ref name="shishi" />
Although it experienced decline after the Meiji Restoration, Renjō-ji has since been restored, with an expanded cemetery and maintained temple grounds.<ref name="shishi" /> In 1964, a large {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 観音菩薩}} statue was erected to honor war casualties.<ref name="shishi" />
== Grounds ==
The temple features a main gate, a kitchen building to the left, and a guest hall beyond the central gate.<ref name="shishi" /> Several stone statues of ancient Buddhas are visible near the central gate and to the right of the main hall.<ref name="shishi" />
The central object of worship is a white, unclothed standing statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], adorned with a fabric hakama. This sacred image is unveiled annually in May.<ref name="shishi" /><ref name="heibon" />
Flanking the main deity are standing statues of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] (Heian period) and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Seishi Bosatsu]] (Muromachi period), both designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].<ref name="heibon" /><ref name="shishi" />
== Cultural Properties ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Renjō-ji (Nishikiderachō, Nara)]][[ja:璉珹寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Renjō-ji (Nishikiderachō, Nara)]][[ja:璉珹寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Renjō-ji (Nishikiderachō, Nara)]][[ja:璉珹寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Nara Renjoji.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Tendai|WD=Q855964}}|prefecture={{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.67333333333333|N|135.8331388888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22120877]] Wikidata name: Renjō-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:25, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} == Renjō-ji (璉珹寺) ==
'''Renjō-ji''' (璉珹寺) is a temple of the {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect|ja|浄土真宗遣迎院派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect|1=Jōdo Shinshū Kengōin-ha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556825}} located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan.
== History ==
According to temple records, Renjō-ji was founded during the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] under the imperial decree of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. It was later rebuilt in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] by {{ill|Ki no Aritsune|ja|紀有常|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Aritsune|1=Ki no Aritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605067}}.<ref name="kenshi">Iwakiri Takatoshi, Oya Yoshinori, eds., "Nara Prefecture History Vol. 6: Temples," Meicho Shuppan, 1991, p.273.</ref><ref name="shishi">Nara City History Editorial Council, "Nara City History: Temples Edition," Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1985, p.125.</ref>
It is also believed to be associated with the remains of {{ill|Ki-dera Temple|ja|紀寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki-dera Temple|1=kinotera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605022}}, evidenced by the discovery of early Nara period tiles in the surrounding area.<ref name="shishi" />
In 1602, [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted Renjō-ji land worth 20 koku in Chōtsuka and Hokkeji villages, a temple domain maintained throughout the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].<ref name="kenshi" /><ref name="heibon">"Japanese Historical Place Names Series Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture," Heibonsha, 1981, p.520.</ref>
The temple was initially affiliated with the [[:en:East Asian Yogācāra|Hossō sect]], later becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Seigan-ji|ja|誓願寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seigan-ji|1=Seigan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11028481}} of the [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo sect]]. In 1725, it converted to [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Buddhism]], becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Yōgen-in|ja|養源院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōgen-in|1=Yōgen-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666843}} in Kyoto.<ref name="shishi" /> Currently, Renjō-ji belongs to the Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect and operates without parishioners.<ref name="shishi" />
Although it experienced decline after the Meiji Restoration, Renjō-ji has since been restored, with an expanded cemetery and maintained temple grounds.<ref name="shishi" /> In 1964, a large {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 観音菩薩}} statue was erected to honor war casualties.<ref name="shishi" />
== Grounds ==
The temple features a main gate, a kitchen building to the left, and a guest hall beyond the central gate.<ref name="shishi" /> Several stone statues of ancient Buddhas are visible near the central gate and to the right of the main hall.<ref name="shishi" />
The central object of worship is a white, unclothed standing statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], adorned with a fabric hakama. This sacred image is unveiled annually in May.<ref name="shishi" /><ref name="heibon" />
Flanking the main deity are standing statues of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] (Heian period) and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Seishi Bosatsu]] (Muromachi period), both designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].<ref name="heibon" /><ref name="shishi" />
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] * Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Seishi Bosatsu]]
=== Other Cultural Properties ===
* Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] (designated tangible cultural property of Nara Prefecture)
== Related Sites ==
* {{ill|Ki-dera|ja|紀寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki-dera|1=kinotera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605022}} * {{ill|Sudo Tennō-sha|ja|崇道天皇社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudo Tennō-sha|1=Sudō Tennō-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119116}} (Guardian Shrine) * {{ill|Shōgaku-ji (Nara)|ja|正覚寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgaku-ji (Nara)|1=Shōkaku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545636}} (Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect) * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}
== References ==

== External Links ==

Although it experienced decline after the Meiji Restoration, Renjō-ji has since been restored, with an expanded cemetery and maintained temple grounds.<ref name="shishi" /> In 1964, a large {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 観音菩薩}} statue was erected to honor war casualties.<ref name="shishi" />
== Grounds ==
The temple features a main gate, a kitchen building to the left, and a guest hall beyond the central gate.<ref name="shishi" /> Several stone statues of ancient Buddhas are visible near the central gate and to the right of the main hall.<ref name="shishi" />
The central object of worship is a white, unclothed standing statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], adorned with a fabric hakama. This sacred image is unveiled annually in May.<ref name="shishi" /><ref name="heibon" />
Flanking the main deity are standing statues of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] (Heian period) and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Seishi Bosatsu]] (Muromachi period), both designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].<ref name="heibon" /><ref name="shishi" />
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] * Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Seishi Bosatsu]]
=== Other Cultural Properties ===
* Wooden Standing Statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] (designated tangible cultural property of Nara Prefecture)

'''Renjō-ji''' (璉珹寺) is a temple of the {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect|ja|浄土真宗遣迎院派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect|1=Jōdo Shinshū Kengōin-ha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556825}} located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan.
== History ==
According to temple records, Renjō-ji was founded during the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō era]] under the imperial decree of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. It was later rebuilt in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] by {{ill|Ki no Aritsune|ja|紀有常|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Aritsune|1=Ki no Aritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605067}}.<ref name="kenshi">Iwakiri Takatoshi, Oya Yoshinori, eds., "Nara Prefecture History Vol. 6: Temples," Meicho Shuppan, 1991, p.273.</ref><ref name="shishi">Nara City History Editorial Council, "Nara City History: Temples Edition," Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1985, p.125.</ref>
It is also believed to be associated with the remains of {{ill|Ki-dera Temple|ja|紀寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki-dera Temple|1=kinotera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605022}}, evidenced by the discovery of early Nara period tiles in the surrounding area.<ref name="shishi" />
In 1602, [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted Renjō-ji land worth 20 koku in Chōtsuka and Hokkeji villages, a temple domain maintained throughout the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].<ref name="kenshi" /><ref name="heibon">"Japanese Historical Place Names Series Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture," Heibonsha, 1981, p.520.</ref>
The temple was initially affiliated with the [[:en:East Asian Yogācāra|Hossō sect]], later becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Seigan-ji|ja|誓願寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seigan-ji|1=Seigan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11028481}} of the [[:en:Jōdo-shū|Jōdo sect]]. In 1725, it converted to [[:en:Tiantai|Tendai Buddhism]], becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Yōgen-in|ja|養源院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōgen-in|1=Yōgen-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666843}} in Kyoto.<ref name="shishi" /> Currently, Renjō-ji belongs to the Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect and operates without parishioners.<ref name="shishi" />
Although it experienced decline after the Meiji Restoration, Renjō-ji has since been restored, with an expanded cemetery and maintained temple grounds.<ref name="shishi" /> In 1964, a large {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 観音菩薩}} statue was erected to honor war casualties.<ref name="shishi" />
== Grounds ==
The temple features a main gate, a kitchen building to the left, and a guest hall beyond the central gate.<ref name="shishi" /> Several stone statues of ancient Buddhas are visible near the central gate and to the right of the main hall.<ref name="shishi" />
The central object of worship is a white, unclothed standing statue of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], adorned with a fabric hakama. This sacred image is unveiled annually in May.<ref name="shishi" /><ref name="heibon" />
Flanking the main deity are standing statues of [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Kannon Bodhisattva]] (Heian period) and [[:en:Mahasthamaprapta|Seishi Bosatsu]] (Muromachi period), both designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].<ref name="heibon" /><ref name="shishi" />

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Renjō-ji (Nishikiderachō, Nara)]][[ja:璉珹寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Renjō-ji (Nishikiderachō, Nara)]][[ja:璉珹寺]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Renjō-ji (Nishikiderachō, Nara)]][[ja:璉珹寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Nara Renjoji.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Tendai|WD=Q855964}}|prefecture={{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.67333333333333|N|135.8331388888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22120877]] Wikidata name: Renjō-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:25, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} == Renjō-ji (璉珹寺) ==
'''Renjō-ji''' (璉珹寺) is a temple of the {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect|ja|浄土真宗遣迎院派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect|1=Jōdo Shinshū Kengōin-ha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556825}} located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan.
== History ==
According to temple records, Renjō-ji was founded during the {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō era}} under the imperial decree of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}} by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}}. It was later rebuilt in the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} by {{ill|Ki no Aritsune|ja|紀有常|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Aritsune|1=Ki no Aritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605067}}.<ref name="kenshi">Iwakiri Takatoshi, Oya Yoshinori, eds., "Nara Prefecture History Vol. 6: Temples," Meicho Shuppan, 1991, p.273.</ref><ref name="shishi">Nara City History Editorial Council, "Nara City History: Temples Edition," Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1985, p.125.</ref>
It is also believed to be associated with the remains of {{ill|Ki-dera Temple|ja|紀寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki-dera Temple|1=kinotera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605022}}, evidenced by the discovery of early Nara period tiles in the surrounding area.<ref name="shishi" />
In 1602, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted Renjō-ji land worth 20 koku in Chōtsuka and Hokkeji villages, a temple domain maintained throughout the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}.<ref name="kenshi" /><ref name="heibon">"Japanese Historical Place Names Series Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture," Heibonsha, 1981, p.520.</ref>
The temple was initially affiliated with the {{ill|East Asian Yogācāra|en|East Asian Yogācāra|WD=Q349849|lt=Hossō sect}}, later becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Seigan-ji|ja|誓願寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seigan-ji|1=Seigan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11028481}} of the {{ill|Jōdo-shū|en|Jōdo-shū|WD=Q912208|lt=Jōdo sect}}. In 1725, it converted to {{ill|Tiantai|en|Tiantai|WD=Q668188|lt=Tendai Buddhism}}, becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Yōgen-in|ja|養源院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōgen-in|1=Yōgen-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666843}} in Kyoto.<ref name="shishi" /> Currently, Renjō-ji belongs to the Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect and operates without parishioners.<ref name="shishi" />
Although it experienced decline after the Meiji Restoration, Renjō-ji has since been restored, with an expanded cemetery and maintained temple grounds.<ref name="shishi" /> In 1964, a large {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 観音菩薩}} statue was erected to honor war casualties.<ref name="shishi" />
== Grounds ==
The temple features a main gate, a kitchen building to the left, and a guest hall beyond the central gate.<ref name="shishi" /> Several stone statues of ancient Buddhas are visible near the central gate and to the right of the main hall.<ref name="shishi" />
The central object of worship is a white, unclothed standing statue of {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai}} from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, adorned with a fabric hakama. This sacred image is unveiled annually in May.<ref name="shishi" /><ref name="heibon" />
Flanking the main deity are standing statues of {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q193849|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva}} (Heian period) and {{ill|Mahasthamaprapta|en|Mahasthamaprapta|WD=Q1465683|lt=Seishi Bosatsu}} (Muromachi period), both designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}.<ref name="heibon" /><ref name="shishi" />
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Wooden Standing Statue of {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q193849|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva}} * Wooden Standing Statue of {{ill|Mahasthamaprapta|en|Mahasthamaprapta|WD=Q1465683|lt=Seishi Bosatsu}}
=== Other Cultural Properties ===
* Wooden Standing Statue of {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai}} (designated tangible cultural property of Nara Prefecture)
== Related Sites ==
* {{ill|Ki-dera|ja|紀寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki-dera|1=kinotera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605022}} * {{ill|Sudo Tennō-sha|ja|崇道天皇社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudo Tennō-sha|1=Sudō Tennō-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119116}} (Guardian Shrine) * {{ill|Shōgaku-ji (Nara)|ja|正覚寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgaku-ji (Nara)|1=Shōkaku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545636}} (Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect) * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==
* [https://renjoji.or.jp/ja/ Official Website of Renjō-ji] * [https://yaokami.jp/1295144/ Yaokami - Information on Japanese Shrines and Temples]

'''Renjō-ji''' (璉珹寺) is a temple of the {{ill|Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect|ja|浄土真宗遣迎院派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect|1=Jōdo Shinshū Kengōin-ha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556825}} located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan.
== History ==
According to temple records, Renjō-ji was founded during the {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō era}} under the imperial decree of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}} by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}}. It was later rebuilt in the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} by {{ill|Ki no Aritsune|ja|紀有常|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Aritsune|1=Ki no Aritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605067}}.<ref name="kenshi">Iwakiri Takatoshi, Oya Yoshinori, eds., "Nara Prefecture History Vol. 6: Temples," Meicho Shuppan, 1991, p.273.</ref><ref name="shishi">Nara City History Editorial Council, "Nara City History: Temples Edition," Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1985, p.125.</ref>
It is also believed to be associated with the remains of {{ill|Ki-dera Temple|ja|紀寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki-dera Temple|1=kinotera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605022}}, evidenced by the discovery of early Nara period tiles in the surrounding area.<ref name="shishi" />
In 1602, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted Renjō-ji land worth 20 koku in Chōtsuka and Hokkeji villages, a temple domain maintained throughout the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}.<ref name="kenshi" /><ref name="heibon">"Japanese Historical Place Names Series Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture," Heibonsha, 1981, p.520.</ref>
The temple was initially affiliated with the {{ill|East Asian Yogācāra|en|East Asian Yogācāra|WD=Q349849|lt=Hossō sect}}, later becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Seigan-ji|ja|誓願寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seigan-ji|1=Seigan-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11028481}} of the {{ill|Jōdo-shū|en|Jōdo-shū|WD=Q912208|lt=Jōdo sect}}. In 1725, it converted to {{ill|Tiantai|en|Tiantai|WD=Q668188|lt=Tendai Buddhism}}, becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Yōgen-in|ja|養源院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōgen-in|1=Yōgen-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666843}} in Kyoto.<ref name="shishi" /> Currently, Renjō-ji belongs to the Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect and operates without parishioners.<ref name="shishi" />
Although it experienced decline after the Meiji Restoration, Renjō-ji has since been restored, with an expanded cemetery and maintained temple grounds.<ref name="shishi" /> In 1964, a large {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 観音菩薩}} statue was erected to honor war casualties.<ref name="shishi" />
== Grounds ==
The temple features a main gate, a kitchen building to the left, and a guest hall beyond the central gate.<ref name="shishi" /> Several stone statues of ancient Buddhas are visible near the central gate and to the right of the main hall.<ref name="shishi" />
The central object of worship is a white, unclothed standing statue of {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai}} from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, adorned with a fabric hakama. This sacred image is unveiled annually in May.<ref name="shishi" /><ref name="heibon" />
Flanking the main deity are standing statues of {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q193849|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva}} (Heian period) and {{ill|Mahasthamaprapta|en|Mahasthamaprapta|WD=Q1465683|lt=Seishi Bosatsu}} (Muromachi period), both designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}.<ref name="heibon" /><ref name="shishi" />
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Wooden Standing Statue of {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q193849|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva}} * Wooden Standing Statue of {{ill|Mahasthamaprapta|en|Mahasthamaprapta|WD=Q1465683|lt=Seishi Bosatsu}}
=== Other Cultural Properties ===
* Wooden Standing Statue of {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai}} (designated tangible cultural property of Nara Prefecture)
== Related Sites ==
* {{ill|Ki-dera|ja|紀寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki-dera|1=kinotera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605022}} * {{ill|Sudo Tennō-sha|ja|崇道天皇社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudo Tennō-sha|1=Sudō Tennō-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119116}} (Guardian Shrine) * {{ill|Shōgaku-ji (Nara)|ja|正覚寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgaku-ji (Nara)|1=Shōkaku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545636}} (Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect) * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==
* [https://renjoji.or.jp/ja/ Official Website of Renjō-ji] * [https://yaokami.jp/1295144/ Yaokami - Information on Japanese Shrines and Temples]

It was later rebuilt in the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} by {{ill|Ki no Aritsune|ja|紀有常|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Aritsune|1=Ki no Aritsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605067}}.<ref name="kenshi">Iwakiri Takatoshi, Oya Yoshinori, eds., "Nara Prefecture History Vol. 6: Temples," Meicho Shuppan, 1991, p.273.</ref><ref name="shishi">Nara City History Editorial Council, "Nara City History: Temples Edition," Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1985, p.125.</ref>
It is also believed to be associated with the remains of {{ill|Ki-dera Temple|ja|紀寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki-dera Temple|1=kinotera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605022}}, evidenced by the discovery of early Nara period tiles in the surrounding area.<ref name="shishi" />
In 1602, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted Renjō-ji land worth 20 koku in Chōtsuka and Hokkeji villages, a temple domain maintained throughout the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}.<ref name="kenshi" /><ref name="heibon">"Japanese Historical Place Names Series Vol. 30: Nara Prefecture," Heibonsha, 1981, p.520.</ref>
The temple was initially affiliated with the {{ill|East Asian Yogācāra|en|East Asian Yogācāra|WD=Q349849|lt=Hossō sect|1=East Asian Yogācāra}}, later becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Seigan-ji|ja|誓願寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seigan-ji|1=Seigan-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11028481}} of the {{ill|Jōdo-shū|en|Jōdo-shū|WD=Q912208|lt=Jōdo sect|1=Jōdo-shū}}. In 1725, it converted to {{ill|Tiantai|en|Tiantai|WD=Q668188|lt=Tendai Buddhism|1=Tiantai}}, becoming a sub-temple of {{ill|Yōgen-in|ja|養源院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōgen-in|1=Yōgen-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11666843}} in Kyoto.<ref name="shishi" /> Currently, Renjō-ji belongs to the Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect and operates without parishioners.<ref name="shishi" />
Although it experienced decline after the Meiji Restoration, Renjō-ji has since been restored, with an expanded cemetery and maintained temple grounds.<ref name="shishi" /> In 1964, a large {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 観音菩薩}} statue was erected to honor war casualties.<ref name="shishi" />
== Grounds ==
The temple features a main gate, a kitchen building to the left, and a guest hall beyond the central gate.<ref name="shishi" /> Several stone statues of ancient Buddhas are visible near the central gate and to the right of the main hall.<ref name="shishi" />
The central object of worship is a white, unclothed standing statue of {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai|1=Amitābha}} from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, adorned with a fabric hakama. This sacred image is unveiled annually in May.<ref name="shishi" /><ref name="heibon" />
Flanking the main deity are standing statues of {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q193849|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva|1=Avalokiteśvara}} (Heian period) and {{ill|Mahasthamaprapta|en|Mahasthamaprapta|WD=Q1465683|lt=Seishi Bosatsu|1=Mahasthamaprapta}} (Muromachi period), both designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}.<ref name="heibon" /><ref name="shishi" />
== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Wooden Standing Statue of {{ill|Avalokiteśvara|en|Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q193849|lt=Kannon Bodhisattva|1=Avalokiteśvara}} * Wooden Standing Statue of {{ill|Mahasthamaprapta|en|Mahasthamaprapta|WD=Q1465683|lt=Seishi Bosatsu|1=Mahasthamaprapta}}
=== Other Cultural Properties ===
* Wooden Standing Statue of {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai|1=Amitābha}} (designated tangible cultural property of Nara Prefecture)
== Related Sites ==
* {{ill|Ki-dera|ja|紀寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki-dera|1=kinotera|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605022}} * {{ill|Sudo Tennō-sha|ja|崇道天皇社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sudo Tennō-sha|1=Sōdō Emperor Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22119116}} (Guardian Shrine) * {{ill|Shōgaku-ji (Nara)|ja|正覚寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgaku-ji (Nara)|1=Shōkaku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545636}} (Jōdo Shinshū Kenkyō-in Sect) * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Nara)|ja|御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Nara)|qq=}}

== External Links ==
